titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edward Robie Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School and Yale Divinity Library staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 22:21

did.abstract: The collection documents Robie's professional work at Andover Theological Seminary and as a Congregational minister in Greenland, New Hampshire from 1852 to 1917.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Edward Robie was born in Gorham, Maine in 1821. He graduated from Bowdoin College in 1840 and from Andover Theological Seminary in 1843. He studied in Germany during the next three years, at the universities of Halle and Berlin. When he returned to the U.S. he taught ancient and modern languages at Gorham Academy, 1846-1848. He returned to Andover to become Librarian and Instructor of Sacred Literature at the Seminary, positions he held until 1851. Robie was ordained to the Congregational ministry at Greenland, NH, on February 25, 1852 and remained as pastor of the Greenland church until his death in 1917.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was given to Andover Newton Theological School by the Community Congregational Church of Greenland, New Hampshire in 1987 and transferred to the Yale Divinity Library in 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edward Robie Papers (RG 293), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Edward Robie Papers document his professional work. Almost all material is undated, but it could be presumed that the manuscripts span his pastoral career at Greenland, NH, 1852-1917. The collection includes an autobiography (1868), European travel journal (1896), letters (2 items), sermons (arranged by biblical text) addresses, unpublished literary manuscripts, and book reviews (arranged in alphabetical order).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into seven series:

did.unittitle: Edward Robie to the Congregational Church and Society in Greenland, N.H

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by scriptural text. For a full listing of texts, see the original finding aid for the collection, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: Expository Address on the Sermon on the Mount
Some Lessons of History, to the Members of the Bradeshill(?) Academy
Some Lessons of History, to the Pascataqua Club
Day of Public Fasting and Prayer
Divinity of Christ and His Eternal Dependence Upon the Father
Genesis: An Outline Study of Man
Genesis: Man in the Image of God
Look Aloft: A Meditation at Sea
There is a Wideness in God's Mercy (poem)
The Value of the Old Testament
Briggs, Charles Augustus. The Ethical Teachings of Jesus and Whither? A Theological Question
Darwin, Charles. The Origin of Species by Means of Natural Selection
Hanna, William. Memoir of Life and Writings of Thomas Chalmers
Harnack, Adolf von. Wesen der Christenthismus
Liddon, Henry Parry. The Divinity of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ
Root, Jean.Christie. Edward Irving: Man, Preacher, Prophet
